query: Click any element and ClickSay instantly captures it complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: ClickSay: Click any element and ClickSay instantly captures it | Product Hunt
results.content:
So I built ClickSay. Press Cmd+Shift+K (or change it to any shortcut you like), click one or more elements (Use shift key for more than one), say what you want fixed (in any of the 20 supported languages), or changed, or even a large enhancement. It grabs the CSS selector, computed styles, HTML, (lot of options to add in the sidebar like the screenshot with the element highlighted, and even the React/Vue/Svelte component name). All of that gets packaged into a structured prompt and lands on your clipboard.

Paste it into whatever AI tool you use. It gets the full picture and nails the fix first try. [...] Shem Ojwang

Perfect. This might have been just the solution I have been looking for all along to deal with stubborn code. Does it only copy CSS code, or does it also do JavaScript?

Fred L.
ClickSay

ClickSay

@shemojs Great question. It captures way more than just CSS. When you click an element, ClickSay grabs the CSS selector, computed styles, the actual HTML markup, and with Pro it also captures a screenshot with the element highlighted, the React/Vue/Svelte component name and file path, and even an accessibility audit. [...] So if the issue is JavaScript-related, the AI tool you paste into (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) gets enough context from the selector + HTML + component name to find and fix the JS too. You're basically giving the AI a precise pointer to the exact element and saying "fix this" - it handles the rest whether it's CSS, JS, or both.

Try it out and let me know how it goes with that stubborn code!

Shem Ojwang

@fredmaker Cool. I'm definitely trying this.

Tod

Really like this idea. When vibe-coding frontend, its definitely a lot of effort trying to describe your changes to the agent. The click + dictate approach is a cool concept to get best of both worlds.

Fred L.
ClickSay

ClickSay

@tteer Thanks! It's accurate and really quick.

Abhiranjan kumar

results.title: ClickSay - Chrome Extension
results.content:
How It Works

## Talk to your AI about what's on screen

Stop typing vague descriptions. Click the element, say what's wrong, and your AI gets the full picture.

1

### Click Any Element

Press `Cmd+Shift+K` and click anything on the page. ClickSay captures the selector, styles, HTML, screenshot, and component name.

2

### Speak Your Feedback

The mic starts automatically. Say "make this bigger" or "fix the padding." Just talk - like you'd explain it to a coworker.

3

### AI Gets It Right

A structured prompt with full context lands on your clipboard. Paste into Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Lovable, Replit, or Bolt.new. The AI knows exactly what you're talking about.

ClickSay tutorial video [...] ClickSay tutorial video

Step 1 - Click any element and say your fix with voice feedback popup Captures selector, styles, HTML, and screenshot

Step 2 - ClickSay side panel with element details, pills, and screenshot Full context organized in one place

Step 3 - Paste structured prompt into Claude Code terminal AI gets it right the first time

Features

## Give your AI the context it's been missing

ClickSay captures everything your AI needs to make the right change - automatically.

### Voice-First Workflow

Speak naturally - the mic activates on click and captures your feedback in real time. No typing, no screenshots, no copying selectors.

### Direct Send to AI [...] We're live on Product Hunt! Use code PRODUCTHUNT2026 for 3 months of Pro free Redeem offer →

"Make this bigger" "Fix the spacing"

results.title: ClickSay Reviews - What's Your Honest Take?
results.content:
The honest feedback helps us way more than generic praise - whether it's "this saved me hours" or "this feature is janky" or "I wish it did X instead." We're reading everything and planning our next updates based on what people actually need.

If you haven't left a review yet, it takes like 2 minutes and genuinely makes a difference for other makers finding the extension. Plus it helps us understand if we're solving the real problems people have.

Check it out here if you want to add your thoughts - 

What's been the most useful part of ClickSay for your workflow so far, or what would actually make you use it more?
